requirements: A credible experiment benchmarking unsupervised outlier detectors (IsolationForest, LocalOutlierFactor, OneClassSVM, EllipticEnvelope, or equivalents) with injected anomalies: methods are implemented as distinct code paths, runs cover multiple datasets across anomaly rates, and results address H1/H2/H3 directionally.
judging_note: Score on scientific substance and directional correctness of evidence, not on exact numeric thresholds. Well-motivated substitutes (e.g., HBOS, KNN-based outlier) that test the same scientific question should be credited.

sub_tasks.requirements: The outlier-detection conditions and anomaly-injection pipeline are implemented correctly.

The submission implements multiple distinct detector code paths — typically including IsolationForest, LOF, OneClassSVM, and an elliptic/Gaussian-envelope method — rather than aliases to one shared estimator.
A reproducible anomaly-injection routine is implemented for test data at one or more explicit contamination rates with binary ground-truth anomaly labels.
The code uses multiple datasets (sklearn built-ins or comparable) and applies a consistent preprocessing pipeline (including feature scaling) before model fitting.
Execution records anomaly-detection metrics for each condition.
Execution produces a machine-readable metrics artifact containing numeric ROC-AUC and PR-AUC (or equivalents) for each implemented method on at least one (dataset, rate) cell.
Reported results aggregate over multiple random seeds and include at least two anomaly rates, with a dispersion measure. Honest small-seed runs with variance reported are preferable to a single run.
The run logs a wall-clock timing measure per method and demonstrates completion within a CPU-only workflow.
The submission compares mean ROC-AUC of IsolationForest vs OneClassSVM per dataset and conveys whether IsolationForest is meaningfully better on most datasets — judge directionally against H1.

The submission conveys whether LOF is competitive on PR-AUC for at least one dataset at a higher anomaly rate (H2) and whether detectors meaningfully outperform a random baseline on pooled ROC-AUC (H3).

The README or writeup describes setup and anomaly injection, reports ROC-AUC/PR-AUC results, conveys per-hypothesis outcomes (supported / refuted / inconclusive), and discusses limitations (synthetic anomalies, dataset scope, hyperparameter sensitivity, seed count). No strict word-count requirement.
